title: rubus magurensis
intro: Rubus magurensis, also known as the Korean black raspberry, is a species of thorny shrub native to East Asia. It is known for its dark purple fruits and leaves with medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, providing several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer properties. Rubus magurensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional medicine and food products, such as jams and wines.
